Program 6: Sort a given set of elements using the Merge Sort method and determine the time required to sort the elements. Plot a graph of number of elements versus time taken. Specify the time efficiency class of this algorithm.

import java.util.Scanner;
import java.util.Random;

public class MergeSort {
	static final int MAX = 50005;
	static int[] a = new int[MAX];
	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub
		Scanner put = new Scanner(System.in);
		System.out.print("Enter Max array size: ");
		int n = put.nextInt();
		Random random = new Random();
		System.out.println("Enter the array elements: ");
		for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
		a[i] = random.nextInt(1000); // generate random numbers – uniform 
		 // distribution
		System.out.println("Input Array:");
		for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
		System.out.print(a[i] + " ");
		// set start time
		long startTime = System.nanoTime();
		MergeSortAlgorithm(0, n - 1);
		long stopTime = System.nanoTime();
		long elapsedTime = stopTime - startTime;
		System.out.println("\nSorted Array:");
		for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
		System.out.print(a[i] + " ");
		System.out.println();
		System.out.println("Time Complexity in ms for n=" + n + " is: " + (double) 
		 elapsedTime / 1000000);
	}
	
	public static  void MergeSortAlgorithm(int low, int high) 
	{
		int mid;
		if (low < high) 
		{
			mid = (low + high) / 2;
			MergeSortAlgorithm(low, mid);
			MergeSortAlgorithm(mid + 1, high);
			Merge(low, mid, high);
		}
	}



	public static void Merge(int low, int mid, int high) 
	{
		int[] b = new int[MAX];
		int i, j;
		i = low;
		int k=low;
		j = mid + 1;
	
		while ((i <= mid) && (j <= high))
		{
			if (a[i] < a[j])
			{
				b[k++] = a[i++];
			}
			else
				b[k++] = a[j++];
		}
	
		while(i<=mid)
			b[k++] = a[i++];
		
		while(j<=high)
			b[k++] = a[j++];
	
		for (k = low; k <= high; k++)
			a[k] = b[k];
	}

}
 
Output:
Enter Max array size: 5
Enter the array elements: 
Input Array:
547 91 907 239 531 
Sorted Array:
91 239 531 547 907 
Time Complexity in ms for n=5 is: 0.384



	 
Program 7: Sort a given set of elements using Quick Sort method and determine the time required to sort the elements. Plot a graph of number of elements versus time taken. Specify the time efficiency class of this algorithm.

import java.util.Scanner;
import java.util.Random;

public class QickSortComplexity {

	static final int MAX = 50005;
	static int[] a = new int[MAX];

	public static void main(String[] args) {
			
		Scanner input = new Scanner(System.in);
		System.out.print("Enter Max array size: ");
		int n = input.nextInt();
		Random random = new Random();
		System.out.println("Enter the array elements: ");
		for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
		a[i] = random.nextInt(1000); // generate random numbers – uniform distribution
		System.out.println("Input Array:");
		for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
		{
			System.out.print(a[i] + " ");
		}
		long startTime = System.nanoTime(); // set start time
		QuickSortAlgorithm(0, n - 1);
		long stopTime = System.nanoTime(); // set stop time
		long elapsedTime = stopTime - startTime;
		System.out.println("\nSorted Array:");
		for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
		{
			System.out.print(a[i] + " ");
		}
		System.out.println();
		System.out.println("Time Complexity in ms for n=" + n + " is: " + (double) 
		 elapsedTime / 1000000);
		}

		public static void QuickSortAlgorithm(int L, int H) 
		{
			int i, j, temp, pivot;
			if (L < H) 
			{
				i = L;
				j = H + 1;
				pivot = a[L]; // mark first element as pivot
				while (true) 
				{
					i++;
					while (a[i] <=pivot && i<H)
					{
							i++;
					}
					j--;
					while (a[j] > pivot)
					{
						j--;
					}
					if (i < j) 
					{
						temp = a[i];
						a[i] = a[j];
						a[j] = temp;
					} 
					else
						break; // partition is over
				}
				a[L] = a[j];
				a[j] = pivot;
				QuickSortAlgorithm(L, j - 1);
				QuickSortAlgorithm(j + 1, H);

		}
	}
}

Output:
Enter the number of elements to be sorted
5
Array elements to be sorted are: 
6710 
424 
2583 
1244 
7861 

The sorted elements are: 
424 
1244 
2583 
6710 
7861 

The time taken to sort is 5900ns

Program 8: Implement Topological sort using source removal method find the time required to sort the elements.

import java.util.*;

public class Tsort {

	
	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub

		int i,j,k,n;
		int a[][]=new int[10][10];
		int indeg[]=new int[10],flag[]=new int[10],count=0;

	//indegree-->Stores indegree of each vertex
	//flag-->Marks whether a vertex is already included in topological order

		Scanner sc= new Scanner(System.in);
		System.out.println("Enter the no of vertices:\n");
		n=sc.nextInt();
 
		System.out.println("Enter the adjacency matrix:\n");
		for(i=0;i<n;i++){
			System.out.print("Enter row "+(i+1)+":");
			for(j=0;j<n;j++)
				a[i][j]=sc.nextInt();
		}
 
		for(i=0;i<n;i++){
			indeg[i]=0; //initially assume no incoming edges.
			flag[i]=0; //vertex not yet printed.
		}
 
		//Calculating Indegree of Each Vertex. 
		//Indegree = number of incoming edges to a vertex.
		for(i=0;i<n;i++)
			for(j=0;j<n;j++)
				indeg[i]=indeg[i]+a[j][i];
 
		System.out.println("\nThe topological order is:");
		long startTime = System.nanoTime();
 		while(count<n)//Loop runs until all vertices are processed.
		{
			for(k=0;k<n;k++)
			{
				//Find Vertex with Indegree 0 and Such vertex is 				  printed in topological order
				//indeg[k] == 0 → no incoming edges
				//flag[k] == 0 → not already printed
				if((indeg[k]==0) && (flag[k]==0)){
					System.out.println(" "+(k+1));
					flag [k]=1;
		
 				//After removing a vertex from the graph,Reduce 				  Indegree of Adjacent Vertices
				for(i=0;i<n;i++){
					if(a[k][i]==1)
						indeg[i]--;
				}
			   }
			}
 
			count++;//Tracks how many iterations occurred
		}
		long endTime = System.nanoTime();

        	System.out.println("\nTime taken to sort: " + (endTime - startTime) + 					" nanoseconds");
	}

}

Output:
Enter the no of vertices:

5
Enter the adjacency matrix:

Enter row 1: 0 0 1 0 0
Enter row 2: 0 0 1 0 0
Enter row 3: 0 0 0 1 1
Enter row 4: 0 0 0 0 0
Enter row 5: 0 0 0 0 0

The topological order is:
1
2
3
4
5
